title: Improvement Proposal
trigger: after capability-gap-analysis or component-watch produces actionable findings
description: Draft a structured improvement proposal from gap analysis or component watch findings. Submit for human approval. Never self-modify production.

proposals.title: Kimi K3 — Model Failover Ladder Evaluation
proposals.summary:
Board task doc 1287 identifies Kimi K3 (Moonshot AI, ~2026-07) as 'DIRECTLY relevant to the cheap-AI failover ladder' and asks whether to add it as a coding-loop provider tier via OpenRouter.

Kimi K3 specs: near-frontier on Arena front-end coding (#1), ~$3/M input / $15/M output (roughly Sonnet 5 price), available on OpenRouter. Hallucination rate: 51% (up from 39% in prior Moonshot models). Board task notes 'keep important work on Claude/GPT.'

ZOL's current failover ladder in scripts/zol-daily.js (lines 91-93):
  1. Primary: env OPENROUTER_MODEL or 'anthropic/claude-fable-5'
  2. Tier 2: 'deepseek/deepseek-chat'
  3. Tier 3: 'meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct'

ZOL's model-gateway.js TIER_MODELS (for AgentGateway handlers):
  cheap: anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5
  standard: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 (or OPENROUTER_MODEL)
  frontier: anthropic/claude-opus-4-8 (or ZOL_FRONTIER_MODEL)

KEY CONCERN for ZOL: the daily cast is a FACTUAL use case — 'use ONLY facts in here, do not invent' (zol-daily.js line 86 sys prompt). A 51% hallucination rate would produce false claims about artists, tracks, or events. The existing Telegram approval gate (Zaal reviews before posting) is the safety net, but a hallucinated cast reaching approval is a waste of Zaal's time and risks publication of false music facts.

COMPARISON: deepseek-chat and llama-3.3-70b-instruct are also potentially hallucinatory but are established OpenRouter options. Kimi K3 is newer with a documented higher hallucination rate.

Three candidate placements:
(A) NO CHANGE — skip Kimi K3. High hallucination rate is incompatible with ZOL's factual music-curation use case; deepseek+llama ladder is sufficient. Future evaluation when hallucination rate improves.
(B) ADD TO DAILY FAILOVER — insert 'moonshotai/kimi-k3' between deepseek and llama as a 3rd-tier fallback in scripts/zol-daily.js. The approval gate catches hallucinations; having 4-tier coverage reduces silent-fail risk on credit exhaustion. Low code change (1 line).
(C) ADD AS TIER_MODELS 'budget' — add a 4th tier 'budget' in model-gateway.js TIER_MODELS pointing to moonshotai/kimi-k3 (OpenRouter). Use ONLY for non-factual tasks (creative generation, draft polish) NOT for route/classify/gate-check where correctness is load-bearing.
design_questions: Add Kimi K3 to ZOL's failover ladder? (A: skip / B: daily-failover tier-3 / C: budget tier in TIER_MODELS)
OpenRouter model ID for Kimi K3 — confirmed name: 'moonshotai/kimi-k3'? (needs verification; doc says 'on OpenRouter' but doesn't give model ID)
If Option B: acceptable that Zaal reviews and rejects more hallucinated drafts during credit-exhaustion fallback scenarios?
If Option C: which handlers should route to 'budget' tier vs 'cheap' tier? (suggestion: only artist-spotlight.compose-spotlight-draft and cast.draft, both already human-gated)
proposals.blocked_on: Zaal/Brandon design decision — placement + hallucination risk acceptance
proposals.security_note: No model change may bypass the Telegram approval gate. Any Kimi K3 output goes through the same draft-only → approval → post flow. The 'do not invent' instruction in the system prompt is unchanged. No new credentials required — same OpenRouter API key.

proposals.title: WaveWarZ Receipt Primitive Export
proposals.summary:
Brandon directed (2026-07-20) that WaveWarZ build 'receiptable by design' on ZOL v2 receipt-journal.js as the cryptographic primitive. Every consequential WaveWarZ action (battle, trade, leaderboard calc, winner, settlement, payout, AI commentary, moderation) should emit a verifiable receipt chain: timestamp, actor, wallet, event_id, source_tx, calc_version, parser_version, AI_model_version, prev+current receipt hash = append-only chain of evidence. Brandon notes 'Verified by DreamNet as an optional layer for other platforms' and 'Build into architecture now = easy; retrofit later = hard.'

ZOL v2 already implements the core primitive: receipt-journal.js (SHA-256 hashing, idempotency keys, prev-receipt links), AgentGateway /receipts GET endpoint, and ProofDropAdapter (sanitized evidence bundles). The gap is an external-adoption path — WaveWarZ needs a way to emit receipts in ZOL's schema without tight coupling to the ZOL runtime.

Three candidate designs:
(A) RECEIPT SCHEMA EXPORT — publish a receipt-schema.json (JSON Schema draft-07) that any ZAO platform can validate against locally. Zero new ZOL endpoints; platforms bring their own storage. Minimal scope, immediately actionable.
(B) POST /receipts/verify ENDPOINT — add a receipt submission+verification endpoint to AgentGateway (localhost-only by default, same auth as /tasks). Platforms POST a receipt; ZOL validates schema, checks hash chain integrity, returns {valid, errors}. No cross-platform storage; purely local verification service.
(C) RECEIPT-JOURNAL AS STANDALONE NPM PACKAGE — extract src/receipt-journal.js as an npm package (e.g. @zaoos/receipt-journal) that WaveWarZ (and other ZAO repos) can install directly. Broadest scope; enables 'Verified by DreamNet' without ZOL runtime dependency.
design_questions: Which adoption path? (A: schema export / B: POST /receipts/verify endpoint / C: standalone npm package)
Should ZOL v2 AgentGateway accept cross-platform receipt submissions from non-ZOL callers, or remain ZOL-internal?
Is 'Verified by DreamNet' a public-facing brand claim — and if so, does it require an audit trail that ZOL hosts?
Priority vs. merge train: should this be implemented before or after the #26-#64 merge train completes?
proposals.blocked_on: Zaal/Brandon design decision — adoption path + cross-platform policy
proposals.security_note: No receipt must expose prompts, private memory, credentials, or signer data. ProofDrop redaction rules apply to any export. External receipt submission (Option B) must never bypass the existing Telegram approval gate.

proposals.resolved_by: Brandon directive 2026-07-17 — keep advancing, do not wait idle
proposals.resolution: Option A: AtomicFileStore (conservative default). Capped at 50 drafts; oldest evicted on overflow. Accepts input.type or input.artifactType; stores content/body. Graceful fallback if store unavailable. Retention/read-back remain future work (no artifact.draft.read handler in v2).
proposals.title: Artifact Draft Write — Storage Backend Design
proposals.summary: artifact.draft.write currently returns a draft ID without persisting any content. Three candidate backends: (1) AtomicFileStore keyed by artifactId — simple, already available, no disk-flood protection; (2) capped local FS directory (e.g. ~/.zao/private/artifacts/, rolling 50-file cap) — matches artifact metaphor, human-readable; (3) Zocuments — proper version history, structured, but adds a dependency. Questions for Zaal: preferred backend? max artifact size? retention policy? approval required to read back artifacts?
design_questions: Which storage backend? (AtomicFileStore / local FS directory / Zocuments)
Retention cap (max number of drafts, or TTL)?
Should artifact.draft.read be a paired handler, or is read-back out of scope for v2?
proposals.security_note: Drafts must never be published without the approval gate. artifact.draft.write stays draft-only regardless of backend.

proposals.resolved_by: Brandon directive 2026-07-17 — keep advancing, do not wait idle
proposals.resolution: Option A: Named-preset lookup in handler. WORKOUT_PRESETS table maps workoutScope/workout string → {toolId, inputs, maxRounds}. Presets: scheduled-session (memory.read, 2 rounds), field-test (state.local.read, 3 rounds), tool-workout (receipt.local.query, 3 rounds), mastery-check (cowork.fetch-projects, 1 round). Unknown scope defaults to scheduled-session. Output: {completed, workout, toolId, passed, rounds, timestamp}.
proposals.title: ToolGym Workout Run — Input Shape Decision
proposals.summary: toolgym.workout.run is a stub because loop manifests pass {workout:'field-test'} (a named preset string) while ToolGymAdapter.runWorkout() expects {toolId, inputs, maxRounds}. Three options: (A) named-preset lookup table in handler maps workout name → {toolId, inputs, maxRounds} — keeps simple string in manifests, adds a maintenance table; (B) update loop manifests to pass structured {toolId, inputs, maxRounds} directly — cleaner contract, requires editing tool-workout.manifest.json + mastery-receipt.manifest.json + field-test.manifest.json; (C) accept both shapes — most flexible, doubles handler complexity. All options are safe local-only changes with no external calls or Pi credentials needed.
design_questions: Which input shape? (A: named-preset lookup in handler / B: structured inputs in manifests / C: accept both)
If Option A: should the preset table live in the handler file or a separate config JSON (e.g. loops/toolgym-presets.json)?
Which named workout presets are needed for v2? (e.g. field-test, tool-workout, mastery-check)
Should workout output include per-round results, or only a final pass/fail summary?
proposals.security_note: toolgym.workout.run must never auto-install packages or modify handler files. All workout steps are bounded, local, read/invoke only.
